commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the blocker is a telemetry privacy/default policy decision, not a narrow mechanical repair.; Needs attention: The patch changes a privacy-sensitive telemetry default and could export rendered local log content to external collectors without opt-in.; Review finding: [P1] Keep log bodies behind content capture — extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts:159 (sha=dd3778f9907cf6cb42f80804c8d9e26ddee83e12)
commands.review_summary:
The PR flips `NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE.logBodies` to `true` in the diagnostics-otel plugin default content capture policy.

PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main routes default log export through `NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE`, and `buildDiagnosticLogRecord` emits body `"log"` unless log-body capture is enabled. I did not run a live Loki collector in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the blocker is a telemetry privacy/default policy decision, not a narrow mechanical repair.

target.title: fix(otel): set logBodies=true in default content capture policy
target.body:
Fixes #95633

## Summary

OTLP log records exported by the diagnostics-otel plugin arrived in Loki with `Body` set to the literal string `"log"` because the default content capture policy (`NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE`) had `logBodies: false`.

## Root Cause

`recordLogRecord` at line 1796:

```typescript
const body = shouldCaptureOtelLogBody(contentCapturePolicy)
  ? normalizeOtelLogString(evt.message || "log", MAX_OTEL_LOG_BODY_CHARS)
  : "log";
```

When `logBodies` is `false`, body is hardcoded to `"log"`. The default policy had `logBodies: false`, affecting all users without explicit content capture config.

## Changes

- `extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts`: Change `NO_CONTENT_CAPTURE.logBodies` from `false` to `true`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: OTLP log bodies now contain the actual log message text instead of the literal string "log".

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
npx tsx -e '
const fs = require("fs");
const src = fs.readFileSync("./extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts", "utf-8");
console.log("logBodies default:", src.includes("logBodies: true") ? "✅ true" : "❌ false");
console.log("Body uses evt.message:", src.includes("normalizeOtelLogString(evt.message") ? "✅" : "❌");
'
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```text
$ npx tsx -e '
const fs = require("fs");
const src = fs.readFileSync("./extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts", "utf-8");
console.log("logBodies default:", src.includes("logBodies: true") ? "✅ true" : "❌ false");
console.log("Body uses evt.message:", src.includes("normalizeOtelLogString(evt.message") ? "✅" : "❌");
'
logBodies default: ✅ true
Body uses evt.message: ✅

Before: logBodies: false → body always "log"
After:  logBodies: true  → body = evt.message (actual log text)
```

**What was not tested**: End-to-end with a real OTLP collector + Loki setup (requires external infrastructure). The change is a boolean flip in the default policy.

## Risk

- [x] Backwards compatible (explicit configs unaffected)
- [x] Minimal change (1 line)

Closes: #95633
target.author: ajwan8998
